name: Kottopoula Me Pilafi (Chicken W/pilaf)
recipeIngredient: 1 1/2 pounds Tomatoes, peeled and strained OR
2 1/3 cups Water for each cup of rice
recipeInstructions:
1. Wash and wipe the chicken.
2. Brown butter in a large pot.
3. Add chicken to the pot and brown on all sides.
4. Add onion and let it brown.
5. Add tomatoes (or diluted tomato paste) and 1 cup of water.
6. Cook for about 20 minutes.
7. Add 2 1/3 cups of water for each cup of rice you will use, then continue cooking until the chicken is tender (about 20 to 30 minutes).
8. Remove chicken parts with a slotted spoon and keep them hot.
9. Add rice to the sauce in the pot, stirring at first to prevent sticking.
10. Simmer, covered, until the rice is tender and has absorbed most of the sauce (about 20 minutes).
11. Remove from heat.
12. Place chicken on top of the rice, cover the pot with a clean towel, and put the lid back over the towel. Let stand for 10 minutes before serving.
